```{r, echo=FALSE, results="hide"}
repeat {
  fl <- sample(seq(0.10,0.60,0.05),1); jump <- sample(seq(0.10,0.35,0.05),1)
  fx <- fl+jump; x0 <- sample(-2:4,1)
  vals <- round(c(jump,fx,fl,1-fx,fx+fl),3)
  if (fx <= 1 && length(unique(vals)) == 5L) break
}
opts <- sprintf("$%.3f$",vals)
```

Question
========
Uma FDA satisfaz $F_X(`r x0`^-)=`r fl`$ e $F_X(`r x0`)=`r fx`$. Qual é $P(X=`r x0`)$?

Answerlist
----------
* `r opts[1]`
* `r opts[2]`
* `r opts[3]`
* `r opts[4]`
* `r opts[5]`

Solution
========
A probabilidade pontual é o tamanho do salto:
$$P(X=x_0)=F_X(x_0)-F_X(x_0^-)
=`r fx`-`r fl`=`r jump`.$$

Meta-information
================
exname: Cap1S5Q3_salto_FDA
extype: schoice
exsolution: 10000
exshuffle: TRUE
